_meta.description: Clinical suggestion prompts for Aortica AI copilot. These are non-prescriptive decision-support cues — NOT treatment orders. Editable by clinicians without code changes.
_meta.disclaimer: Decision support only — requires clinician judgment

AF.prompt: Evaluate stroke risk (CHA₂DS₂-VASc) and consider anticoagulation
AF.rationale: Atrial fibrillation carries significant thromboembolic risk; CHA₂DS₂-VASc scoring guides anticoagulation decisions and reduces stroke incidence.

AFL.prompt: Cardiology referral recommended for rate/rhythm control
AFL.rationale: Atrial flutter often requires catheter ablation of the cavotricuspid isthmus for definitive treatment and carries similar stroke risk to AF.

SVT.prompt: If symptomatic, consider vagal manoeuvres or adenosine
SVT.rationale: Supraventricular tachycardia is usually haemodynamically stable; vagal manoeuvres are first-line, adenosine for acute termination.

AVNRT.rationale: AV nodal re-entrant tachycardia has high cure rates (>95%) with catheter ablation of the slow pathway.

AVRT.prompt: Electrophysiology referral — assess for accessory pathway
AVRT.rationale: AV re-entrant tachycardia involves an accessory pathway that may carry risk of rapid conduction in AF, warranting risk stratification.

VT.prompt: Urgent cardiology review — assess hemodynamic stability
VT.rationale: Ventricular tachycardia can degenerate to VF; immediate assessment of haemodynamic stability and underlying substrate is critical.

VF.rationale: Ventricular fibrillation is a cardiac arrest rhythm requiring immediate defibrillation; survival decreases ~10% per minute without intervention.

idioventricular.rationale: Accelerated idioventricular rhythm is typically a benign reperfusion arrhythmia that resolves spontaneously.

sinus_brady.prompt: Assess for symptoms; consider reversible causes
sinus_brady.rationale: Sinus bradycardia is often physiologic (athletes, sleep) but may indicate sick sinus syndrome, hypothyroidism, or drug effect.

sinus_tachy.prompt: Evaluate underlying cause (fever, pain, volume status)
sinus_tachy.rationale: Sinus tachycardia is almost always a secondary response; treating the underlying cause is the appropriate intervention.

PAC.prompt: Usually benign — reassurance unless highly symptomatic
PAC.rationale: Premature atrial complexes are common and rarely require treatment; frequent PACs may predict AF development.

PVC.rationale: Frequent PVCs (>10% burden) can cause PVC-induced cardiomyopathy; echocardiography assesses LV function.

av_block_1st.rationale: First-degree AV block is typically an incidental finding; progressive PR prolongation warrants follow-up.

av_block_2nd.prompt: Differentiate Mobitz I vs II — cardiology input if Mobitz II
av_block_2nd.rationale: Mobitz type I (Wenckebach) is usually benign; Mobitz type II carries risk of progression to complete heart block and may require pacing.

av_block_3rd.prompt: Urgent cardiology — temporary pacing may be required
av_block_3rd.rationale: Complete heart block causes haemodynamic compromise and syncope; temporary pacing is often needed as a bridge to permanent pacemaker.

LBBB.prompt: New LBBB warrants acute coronary syndrome workup
LBBB.rationale: New-onset LBBB may represent acute MI (Sgarbossa criteria apply); chronic LBBB requires assessment for underlying cardiomyopathy.

RBBB.prompt: Evaluate for right heart strain if new onset
RBBB.rationale: Isolated RBBB is often benign; new RBBB should prompt evaluation for pulmonary embolism, right heart disease, or conduction disease.

LAFB.prompt: Usually benign — note for trend monitoring
LAFB.rationale: Left anterior fascicular block is common and usually benign; bifascicular block (LAFB + RBBB) warrants closer monitoring.

LPFB.prompt: Rare — consider structural heart disease evaluation
LPFB.rationale: Left posterior fascicular block is uncommon and may indicate underlying structural disease given the robust dual blood supply of the posterior fascicle.

WPW.prompt: Electrophysiology referral — risk stratification for SCD
WPW.rationale: Wolff-Parkinson-White syndrome carries risk of sudden cardiac death if the accessory pathway has short anterograde refractory period during AF.

pacemaker_rhythm.prompt: Verify appropriate pacing capture and sensing
pacemaker_rhythm.rationale: Pacemaker rhythm should be evaluated for appropriate capture, sensing, and programmed parameters at regular intervals.

normal_sinus_rhythm.prompt: Normal sinus rhythm — no specific action needed
normal_sinus_rhythm.rationale: Normal sinus rhythm is the expected baseline rhythm; no intervention is required.

LVH.prompt: Blood pressure optimisation and echo assessment
LVH.rationale: ECG-detected LVH is an independent cardiovascular risk factor; echocardiography quantifies mass and identifies underlying cause.

RVH.prompt: Evaluate for pulmonary hypertension or right heart strain
RVH.rationale: Right ventricular hypertrophy suggests chronically elevated right-sided pressures; pulmonary hypertension workup is indicated.

LVSD.rationale: AI-detected LVSD (subclinical or overt) warrants echocardiographic confirmation and initiation of guideline-directed medical therapy.

HFpEF_risk.prompt: NT-proBNP and exercise testing may clarify diagnosis
HFpEF_risk.rationale: HFpEF is underdiagnosed; NT-proBNP and diastolic stress testing improve diagnostic yield in patients with exertional dyspnoea.

DCM.rationale: Dilated cardiomyopathy requires cardiac MRI for characterisation, genetic testing consideration, and optimisation of heart failure therapy.

HCM.prompt: Family screening and SCD risk assessment
HCM.rationale: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy is the leading cause of SCD in young athletes; first-degree relative screening and risk stratification are essential.

ARVC.prompt: Cardiac MRI and genetic testing recommended
ARVC.rationale: Arrhythmogenic right ventricular cardiomyopathy is diagnosed via modified Task Force criteria requiring imaging, genetics, and electrophysiology data.

amyloidosis.prompt: Consider Tc-PYP scan and haematology referral
amyloidosis.rationale: Cardiac amyloidosis (ATTR or AL) requires prompt subtyping; Tc-PYP scintigraphy is highly specific for ATTR amyloidosis.

aortic_stenosis.rationale: ECG findings suggestive of aortic stenosis (LVH, strain) warrant echo-Doppler to quantify severity and guide intervention timing.

mitral_regurgitation.rationale: Mitral regurgitation severity guides management — moderate-to-severe MR may require surgical or transcatheter intervention.

pulmonary_HTN.prompt: Right heart catheterisation may be needed
pulmonary_HTN.rationale: ECG signs of pulmonary hypertension warrant haemodynamic confirmation via right heart catheterisation for definitive diagnosis and classification.

LA_enlargement.prompt: Evaluate atrial fibrillation risk and valvular disease
LA_enlargement.rationale: Left atrial enlargement is a strong predictor of AF and may indicate significant mitral valve disease or diastolic dysfunction.

RA_enlargement.rationale: Right atrial enlargement suggests right-sided volume or pressure overload from conditions such as tricuspid regurgitation or pulmonary hypertension.

pericarditis.rationale: Pericarditis treatment includes NSAIDs with colchicine adjunct; echocardiography is needed to exclude significant pericardial effusion.

myocarditis.prompt: Cardiac MRI and troponin trend recommended
myocarditis.rationale: Myocarditis requires cardiac MRI for confirmation (Lake Louise criteria) and serial troponins to assess extent of myocardial injury.

STEMI.prompt: Activate cath lab — door-to-balloon time critical
STEMI.rationale: STEMI requires emergent percutaneous coronary intervention; door-to-balloon time <90 minutes is the standard of care.

posterior_MI.prompt: Obtain posterior leads (V7-V9); emergent cath consideration
posterior_MI.rationale: Posterior MI is frequently missed on standard 12-lead ECG; posterior leads confirm the diagnosis and guide emergent reperfusion.

occlusive_NSTEMI.prompt: Serial troponins and urgent cardiology consultation
occlusive_NSTEMI.rationale: Occlusive NSTEMI (OMI) may have a similar prognosis to STEMI; early invasive strategy within 2 hours improves outcomes.

old_MI.prompt: Verify with prior ECGs; optimise secondary prevention
old_MI.rationale: Q waves suggesting old MI should be correlated with clinical history; secondary prevention medications should be optimised.

hyperkalaemia.prompt: Urgent electrolytes — calcium gluconate if severe
hyperkalaemia.rationale: Severe hyperkalaemia causes life-threatening arrhythmias; IV calcium gluconate stabilises the myocardium while definitive treatment is initiated.

hypokalaemia.prompt: Electrolytes warrant checking and potassium replacement
hypokalaemia.rationale: Hypokalaemia prolongs QT interval and increases arrhythmia risk; potassium replacement is indicated when K+ <3.5 mmol/L.

hypercalcaemia.prompt: Check corrected calcium and parathyroid hormone
hypercalcaemia.rationale: ECG changes of hypercalcaemia (short QT) warrant laboratory confirmation; primary hyperparathyroidism and malignancy are the most common causes.

hypothyroidism_pattern.rationale: ECG features suggestive of hypothyroidism (low voltage, bradycardia, T-wave changes) should prompt thyroid function testing.

digitalis_effect.prompt: Check digoxin level — assess for toxicity signs
digitalis_effect.rationale: Digitalis effect (reverse tick ST depression) is expected at therapeutic levels; toxicity causes arrhythmias and requires level monitoring.

QTc_prolongation.rationale: QTc prolongation increases risk of torsades de pointes; medication review and electrolyte correction (Mg²⁺, K⁺) are priority interventions.
